Extract movement trajectories from a calculateStepDistances() result

Source: R/calculateStepDistances.R
getTrajectories.Rd

Retrieves the per-individual movement trajectories (spatial-line geometries) attached by calculateStepDistances to its distance-enriched output.

Usage

getTrajectories(x)

Arguments

x

A distance-enriched data frame returned by calculateStepDistances.

Value

A named list of trajectory geometries (one element per individual), or NULL if the object carries no trajectories.

See also

calculateStepDistances, plotMaps

Examples

data(rays)
rays_dist <- calculateStepDistances(rays)
#> Warning: - 'id.col' converted to factor.
#> 
  |                                                                            
  |                                                                      |   0%Calculating linear paths between consecutive positions...
#> 
  |                                                                            
  |=========                                                             |  12%
  |                                                                            
  |==================                                                    |  25%
  |                                                                            
  |==========================                                            |  38%
  |                                                                            
  |===================================                                   |  50%
  |                                                                            
  |============================================                          |  62%
  |                                                                            
  |====================================================                  |  75%
  |                                                                            
  |=============================================================         |  88%
  |                                                                            
  |======================================================================| 100%
#> Total execution time: 0.05 secs 
traj <- getTrajectories(rays_dist)
length(traj)
#> [1] 8
